content.full_text:
Farn PolrrrcAt- Pnacrrcps C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street o Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-s660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
August 1,2001
Donald E. White
1959 Cornelius Avenue
Rio Oso, CA95674
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-01-149
Dear Mr. White
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-of-
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
QUESTION
You have asked whether you would have a conflict of interest when South Sutter
Recreation Association's ("SSRA") petition to form a community service district within
Sutter County comes before the Local Agency Formation Commission where you serve
as a commissioner.
CONCLUSION
As long as Petitioner Diane Fales and SSRA are not sources of income to you,
you would have no conflict of interest in decisions regarding SSRA's petition.
Additionally, because salary from a local governmental agency is not considered income
under the Act's conflicts-of-interest provisions, no conflict of interest will arise with the
cemetery or reclamation districts.
1 Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commissionregulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 I 09- 1 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-01-149
Page No. 2
FACTS
You serve as a commissioner on the Sutter County Local Agency Formation
Commission. The SSRA, a non-profit organization, is in the process of forming a
community service district within part of Sutter County. Diane Fales is the
administrative assistant for Reclamation District No. 1001 where you are the secretary-
manager. She also serves as secretary for the Fairview Cemetery District where you
serve as a trustee.
You have worked and volunteered for the SSRA in the past. However, you have
not worked for this organization since 1997.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits a public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a governmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest. In order to determine whether a
public official has a conflict of interest under section 87100, Regulation 18700 provides
the following eight-step analysis.
Step One: Are you a oopublic official?"
As a commissioner on the Sutter County Local Agency Formation Commission
you are a "member. . . of a state or local government agency," afld therefore are a "public
official" subject to the conflict of interest provisions of the Act. (Section 82048;
Regulation 18701(a).)
Step Two: Will you be making, participating in making, or influencing a
governmental decision ?
As a member of the Sutter County Local Agency Formation Commission, you
will "make a governmental decision" if you vote on a petition to form a community
service district within Sutter County. (Regulation 18702.1) Additionally, if you make a
recommendation with regard to this petition to the other commissioners or discuss it with
them, you will be "participat[ing] in making" the decision on the petition. (Regulation
t8702.2)
Step Three: Identifying your economic interests involved in the decisions.
The Act's conflict-of-interest provisions apply only to conflicts of interest arising
from economic interests. There are six kinds of economic interests:


a
File No. A-01-149
Page No. 3
A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she has a
direct or indirect' investment of $2,000 or more (Section 87103(a); Regulation
1 8703.1 (a));
A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more (Section 871 03 (b); Regulation 18703 .2);
A public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months prior to the
decision (Section 87103(c); Regulation 18703.3);
A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she is a
director, officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of management
(Section 871 03 (d), Regulation I 8703. 1(b));
A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if the
gifts aggregateto $320 or more within 12 months prior to the decision (Section
871 03 (e); Regulation 187 03.4);
A public official has an economic interest in his or her personal finances (expenses,
income, assets, or liabilities), as well as those of his or her immediate family.
(Section 87 103 ; Regulation 1 8703. 5).
a
o
a
a
a
The SSRA is a potential economic interest to you as source of income. However, in
your letter you stated that you have not worked for SSRA since 1997. On the assumption
ihut yog have not received aggregate income of $500 or more from SSRA within the past
12 months andthatno income is "promised" to you, the SSRA is not an economic
interest to you under the Act. Additionally, you have provided no facts in your letter to
indicate that Mrs. Fales may be a possible source of income to you, or an economic
interest of any other kind.
You have also provided no facts that indicate either the cemetery or reclamation
districts might be affected by the decision regarding SSRA's formation petition. Because
the cemetery *d reclamation districts are both considered local government agencies3,
they would not be considered economic interests to you in any event because any income
you receive from a local government agency is not defined as "income" under the Act.
Ordinarily a governmental entity which would apparently not be affected by a
governmental decision would not be included in a conflicts analysis. However, because
you have specifically inquired about a possible conflict arising under the Act from your
2 An indirect investment or interest in real property means, among other things, any investment
or interest owned by the official's immediate family. (Section 87103')
3 The definition of a local government agency under the Act is "a county, city or district of any kind
including school distric! or any other local or regional political subdivisio& or any department division,
bureau, offlce, board, commission or other agency of the foregoing." (Section 82041).


File No. A-01-149
Page No. 4
positions with the cemetery and reclamation districts, they have been included in the
analysis.
Because you have disclosed to us no economic interests under the Act, and therefore
no conflicts of interest, it is not necessary to continue with the remaining steps of the
analysis.
If you have any other questions regarding this matler, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Kevin Hall
Legal Intern, Legal Divi sion
